paper_title: Geomorphic Interpretation and Sea-Level Fluctuations in W-India

methods.description: This method involves the production of a geomorphic map through the interpretation of landforms and faults using satellite imagery. The purpose is to understand the geomorphological features and structural patterns of the study area.

steps.description: The main map was produced through a remotely sensed geomorphic interpretation of landforms and faults from CARTOSAT-I and Corona satellite products stereo pair with a geometrical resolution of 2.5 and 3 m, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: fetch and interpret satellite imagery for geomorphic mapping

steps.description: We generated a 3D anaglyphs mosaic following the methodology proposed by Evans et al. (2008) and Gianinetto & Fassi (2008).
steps.searchable_summary: create 3D anaglyph mosaic from satellite imagery

steps.description: We depicted the topography along NHR using CORONA stereo images following the methodology proposed by Sahoo & Malik (2017), Arora et al. (2019).
steps.searchable_summary: depict topography using CORONA stereo images

steps.description: In five years, from 2018-2022, we carried out multiple field surveys to validate the achieved outcome regarding lithological contacts, structures, and landforms.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct field surveys to validate geomorphic map

methods.description: This method involves digitizing historical data and plotting it into a georeferenced environment to analyze the location of Harappan settlements, paleo shorelines, and sea levels.

steps.description: The location of the Harappan settlements and paleo shorelines were digitized from literature and plotted into a georeferenced environment.
steps.searchable_summary: digitize and georeference Harappan settlements and paleo shorelines

steps.description: Through a literature review, we digitized the Quaternary-Holocene Sea levels along with plotting the collected data locations into a georeferenced environment.
steps.searchable_summary: digitize and georeference Quaternary-Holocene sea levels

methods.description: This method involves generating a Digital Elevation Model (DEM) and extracting swath profiles to explore relief distribution and seismic events.

steps.description: To explore the KU relief distribution through swath profiles, we generated a DEM with a geometrical resolution of roughly 15 m, following the methodology proposed by Braun (2021).
steps.searchable_summary: generate DEM for relief distribution analysis

steps.description: We extracted four swaths: (1) a 10 km width and 156 km long, running parallel to the NHR, and (2) three 5 km width and 25 km long, running across the NHR to the Banni plain.
Following the baseline of the first profile, we created a 40 km width swath section sampling instrumental and historical seismic events.
steps.searchable_summary: create swath section for seismic event sampling
